comments: Repeated, sustained dry, insect-like trilling. Singing at dusk in thick grassy and flowery cover in sloping hillside meadow. Possibly more than one bird. Recorded.
Low over houses. Fewer than past years I think.
Adult roosting in front of middle hide on old scrapes
Among some velvets. Water was very choppy, and they just wouldn't lift their heads up so couldn't see bill, though there was a distinct patch on the back of the heads.
100ish. Some flocking with other velvets, some with eiders.
Male.  Likely more, but distant and thus hard to identify.
